text: The two of us set out early on a brilliant morning, our destination a singular island far across the Atlantic. The proposal had seemed a bargain at first, an affordable adventure, but the expense soon grew, and a needy budget left us tracking every dollar. Our slender boat, with one oar and a worn amplifier for the radio, was no match for the torrent that would later sweep the deck. A profitable venture, the dealer had called it, though his disreputable, deceitful grin should have warned us. He was an insolvent tinker who would offend anyone, an unaccommodating man whose every word seemed a piece of propaganda. We engaged him only out of restraint, fearing worse. By mid-ocean a nightmare began. A torrent of rain lashed the bulky hull; the boat seemed to dive, then whirl, and I had to choke back a shriek. The worst was a near crash against a reef in the western hemisphere of the chart, where the inaccessible north shore loomed like a veil of mist, a shroud over the unofficial route no captain would carve. My methodical, conscientious companion, ever influential and brilliant in a crisis, drew on empirical skill to steady us. Somehow she could withstand the swell, her toe braced, her face like wool-pale stone. We must endure, she said, and her singular calm did not crumble. I am afraid we strayed badly. The compass, our only mirror of true direction, gave a hideous reading, and a dive into the charts brought a reduction of hope. Still, we did not panic. To dive deeper into the math, she pulled a quarterly almanac, ran the figures twice, and after careful scrutiny found the error. A magnet near the dial had skewed everything. Once we moved it, the needle settled, and a torrent of relief swept over us. By dusk the storm endures no longer; the sweep of clear sky returned. We could already taste the steak and lamb the inhabitants of the island were said to roast. A stewardess on a passing French liner from Portugal waved; we waved back, funny and giddy with survival. Negotiation with the harbor pilot was brisk. We had email, but no signal; instead we relied on an introductory chart and the syllabus of seamanship she had memorized. The originality of her plan, an apologetic blend of art and guesswork, somehow worked. As an ambulance of gulls wheeled overhead, I felt the stimulation of pure joy. We had unified our nerve. No terrorism of the sea, no miniature fear, could now frustrate us. A mouse of doubt still gnawed, but our determination held. The decoration on the prow, a small carved swan, seemed to nod. Crossing that crossing line of latitude, I knew this affair would mark us forever, a memory to venerate, a story no consideration of fatigue could ever erase. Back on the outer dock, we did not stray from our plan, nor did our courage tumble. We would engage no needless confrontation with the sea. A rough sluice gate guarded the inner harbor; beyond it, a baker let yeast rise for our welcome bread. To distil one lesson from the voyage, and to unify our small crew, I would call us survivors. The harbor's loyal clientele, who track every arriving ship, cheered as we tied up.
translation: 我们两人在一个晴朗的清晨早早出发，目的地是一座遥远的、独一无二的岛屿，远在大西洋的另一边。这个提议起初看似划算，是一次买得起的冒险，但开销很快增加，拮据的预算让我们盯紧每一美元。我们那艘细长的小船，只有一支桨和一台破旧的收音机扩大器，根本敌不过后来席卷甲板的激流。那个商人称这是一桩有利可图的买卖，尽管他那声名狼藉、骗人的笑容本该警告我们。他是个无力偿债的焊锅匠，谁都会被他得罪，是个不肯通融的人，每句话都像宣传。我们只是出于克制才雇了他，怕遇到更糟的。航行到大洋中央，噩梦开始了。倾盆大雨抽打着笨重的船体；船一会儿俯冲，一会儿打旋，我不得不强忍住一声尖叫。最糟的是险些撞上海图西半球处的一片礁石，那里难以接近的北岸像一层雾的面纱赫然耸现，是没有哪个船长敢去开辟的非正式航线上的一块遮蔽物。我那有条理、认真的同伴，在危机中一向有影响力又出色，凭借经验的本领稳住了我们。她不知怎地能顶住涌浪，脚趾撑着，脸像羊毛般苍白的石头。我们必须坚持，她说，她那非凡的镇定没有崩溃。恐怕我们偏航得很厉害。指南针——我们唯一能映照真实方向的镜子——给出一个骇人的读数，一头扎进海图带来了希望的减少。但我们没有惊慌。为了更深入地钻研计算，她抽出一本季刊年历，把数字算了两遍，经过仔细审查后找到了错误。表盘附近有块磁铁扭曲了一切。我们一把它挪开，指针就稳定下来，一股如激流般的宽慰席卷我们。黄昏时分，暴风雨不再持续；晴空的连绵又回来了。我们已经仿佛尝到岛上居民据说会烤的牛排和羊羔肉。一艘从葡萄牙驶来的法国客轮上的女乘务员挥手；我们挥手回应，因为劫后余生而又滑稽又眩晕。与港口领航员的谈判很爽快。我们有电子邮件，却没有信号；于是我们依靠一张入门海图和她背下来的航海课程提纲。她那计划的独创性，是一种带着歉意的、艺术与猜测的混合，竟然奏效了。当一群海鸥像救护车般在头顶盘旋时，我感到纯粹喜悦的刺激。我们已把勇气拧成一股。海洋的任何恐怖、任何微小的恐惧，如今都无法挫败我们。一只怀疑的耗子仍在啃噬，但我们的决心未动。船头的装饰物，一只小小的雕刻天鹅，似乎点了点头。越过那条纬度的交叉线，我知道这段经历将永远在我们身上留下印记，一段值得崇敬的记忆，一个任何疲惫的考量都无法抹去的故事。回到外侧的码头，我们没有偏离计划，勇气也没有崩塌。我们不会与大海进行任何无谓的对峙。一道粗糙的水闸守护着内港；闸门那边，一位面包师让酵母为我们的迎宾面包发起来。若要从这趟航行中提炼出一条教训，并把我们这支小船员队伍凝聚成一体，我会称我们为幸存者。港口忠实的常客们追踪每一艘到港的船，在我们系缆时欢呼起来。
